title: Hoveringham (1) Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including Plt Off. Guy Rerenui Dunlop

sources.title: Aircrew Remembered - P/O Dunlop, Lancaster JB125

external_narratives.summary: Detailed historical account of both Hoveringham Lancaster crashes. JB125, piloted by P/O Guy Dunlop of the RNZAF, crashed at approximately 21:50 on 12 January 1945 after completing a three-hour training flight. In poor visibility with persistent drizzle and cloud base no higher than 6,000 feet, the Lancaster banked over a field and flew into the ground three-quarters of a mile north of the Elm Tree Hotel, Hoveringham. All seven crew, with an average age of 23, were killed.
key_details: Crew of three New Zealanders and four British airmen
Crashed three-quarters of a mile from Elm Tree Hotel

external_narratives.summary: IWM registers twin memorial stones at Hoveringham commemorating the crews of both Lancaster JB125 and LM308. The left stone bears the inscription for JB125 crew lost on 12 January 1945.
key_details: Twin memorial stones for both Lancaster crashes

external_narratives.summary: The local parish council documents the Hoveringham Lancaster Bomber Memorial, noting that wreckage was rediscovered in 2009 and the memorial stones were dedicated on 30 May 2010 adjacent to the River Trent at Hoveringham Hall.
key_details: Located adjacent to River Trent at Hoveringham Hall

external_narratives.summary: Discussion thread on the RAF Commands forum providing additional research details about the two Lancaster crashes at Hoveringham, including witness accounts and surviving crew family contacts.
key_details: Community research and family contacts documented
